notes.author: Dr. Jessica Tanaka
notes.preview: 4-week post-discharge follow-up. Walking with cane, NG tube removed 4/15, weight up 9.4 kg from admission nadir. Labs improving across the board. Medicaid approved 4/8/2027. Continue MMF 1.5g BID, prednisone taper, HCQ.

notes.author: Dr. David Park / Dr. Maria Castro / Dr. Sarah Beckman
notes.preview: HD8 daily progress note integrating returned results: muscle biopsy, MSA panel, skin biopsy, echo, MBSS, EMG. Trending labs, feeding tolerance, electrolyte status. Plan to initiate pulse methylprednisolone today and continue MMF + nutrition advance.

notes.author: Dr. Vivian Chen / Dr. Sarah Lo
notes.preview: HD6. Bedside derm consult and second skin biopsy from abdominal 'flaky paint' lesion. Pathology shows interface/vacuolar dermatitis with epidermal cytoplasmic vacuolation, weakly positive lupus band test, absent dermal mucin, negative PAS/GMS/Congo Red. Differential discussed; clinical correlation recommended.

notes.author: Dr. Rebecca Yang / Dr. Marc Albertini
notes.preview: HD5. Open biopsy of right vastus lateralis (bedside, US-guided). Path final 3/22 details CD8+ T-cell endomysial infiltrate, perifascicular atrophy, MHC class I upregulation, C5b-9 sarcolemmal deposition. No rimmed vacuoles; normal sarcoglycan.

notes.author: Dr. Rebecca Yang
notes.preview: HD4. Bedside EMG/NCS. NCS normal (no neuropathy). EMG findings: proximal-predominant fibrillations and positive sharp waves, small short polyphasic MUPs with early recruitment, paraspinal involvement. RNS negative (rules out MG). Right vastus lateralis identified as biopsy target.

notes.author: Eun-Ji Lee, RD, CSP, CNSC
notes.preview: HD4. Nutrition assessment with anthropometrics (BMI 15.7, 30.3% weight loss from UBW), lab review (albumin 1.6, prealbumin 6, micronutrient panel), and physical findings. Cautious enteral feeding plan via NG: Nutren 1.0 starting at 10 kcal/kg/d with daily advance, thiamine before carb load, q6h electrolyte monitoring.

notes.author: Dr. Hannah Park / Dr. Jessica Tanaka
notes.preview: HD3 consult by Dr. Tanaka's team (continuity of care). Reviews interval history, exam, labs, imaging, and prior 2/10 biopsy. Plan: hold pulse steroids pending biopsy + MSA results + clean infection screen; restart MMF via NG; coordinate EMG and muscle biopsy this admission.

notes.author: Carolyn Wu, MS, CCC-SLP / Dr. Jordan Tate
notes.preview: HD3 repeat MBSS. Severe oropharyngeal dysphagia with aspiration of thin liquids (silent aspiration on 1 of 3 swallows), aspiration with chewing of soft solid, mild nasal regurgitation. NPO with NG tube recommended.

notes.author: Dr. David Park / Dr. Maria Castro / Dr. Sarah Beckman
notes.preview: Admission H&P for 23F with active SLE presenting from home in extremis: BMI 15.7 (30.3% weight loss), anasarca, diffuse scaling dermatosis, proximal weakness 2-3/5 with head lag, dysphonia, oral ulcers, mild hypothermia. Labs: Hgb 7.8, WBC 2.30, albumin 1.6, prealbumin 6, CK 2360, anti-dsDNA 48, C3 58, C4 5, multiple electrolyte derangements. NPO, cautious IVF, thiamine, multi-consult workup planned.

notes.author: Dr. Sarah Beckman / Dr. Maria Castro
notes.preview: 4-day admission for workup of proximal weakness + dysphagia + weight loss ending in AMA discharge. MBSS 2/9 showed mild oropharyngeal dysphagia with laryngeal penetration of thin liquids; nectar-thick/pureed diet with chin-tuck. Skin biopsy taken 2/10 (pending). Multidisciplinary consults: rheum, neuro, GI, ID, derm, nutrition. EMG and muscle biopsy scheduled but NOT done due to AMA discharge.

notes.author: Dr. James Tran
notes.preview: 23F with active SLE on HCQ/MMF/prednisone presenting with new oropharyngeal dysphagia + proximal weakness + voice change + 18.9% weight loss (now 41.2 kg). CK 1840, aldolase 22, anti-dsDNA 38, low complements, mild proteinuria, oral ulcer. Admitted for inpatient workup.

notes.author: Dr. Andre Williams
notes.preview: 6-week PCP follow-up. SLE responding well to MMF + HCQ + prednisone taper. Joint pain much better, rash mostly resolved, weight up to 47.2 kg, mood improved. New symptom: occasional bolus dysphagia with dry foods x 2-3 weeks. Bedside swallow screen equivocal. Ordered baseline labs.

notes.author: Dr. Jessica Tanaka
notes.preview: 5-day post-ED urgent re-evaluation. Modest clinical improvement on prednisone 30 mg + restarted HCQ. Added mycophenolate 500 mg BID. Contraception counseling. Strong adherence and access plan with social work.

notes.author: Dr. Priya Shah
notes.preview: 23-year-old woman with UCTD/probable early SLE and Hashimoto's thyroiditis presenting with worsening fatigue, joint pain, weight loss, intermittent rash. Weight 43.8 kg (down 7 kg / 13.8% from baseline). Anti-dsDNA now positive, complements low, mild proteinuria, tri-lineage cytopenia. Recommended admission; patient declined.

notes.author: Dr. Andre Williams
notes.preview: 23-year-old woman with UCTD off HCQ for 5 weeks. Joint pain markedly worse, new photosensitive rash, 4.4 kg weight loss, new mild cytopenia, low albumin, elevated ESR/CRP, PHQ-9 11. Bridged HCQ via low-cost cash pay.

notes.author: Walgreens Patterson Park / Esperanza Health staff
notes.preview: HCQ refill could not be filled — manufacturer PAP renewal denied. Patient out of HCQ since ~8/12/2026.

notes.author: Diana Mercado, LCSW
notes.preview: Medicaid application DENIED 5/29/2026; appeal filed today. PAP applications submitted. Food insecurity worsening. Mental health concerns emerging.

notes.author: Dr. Jessica Tanaka
notes.preview: First specialist evaluation. Assessment: undifferentiated CTD, possible early SLE; co-existing Hashimoto's. Continue HCQ, add low-dose prednisone taper. Hand X-rays show non-erosive synovitis.

notes.author: Dr. Manuel Reyes
notes.preview: 2-week follow-up. Send-out labs returned: anti-Sm, anti-dsDNA, anti-Ro/La, anti-CCP all negative; hep B/C/HIV negative; parvovirus IgM negative; TPO antibody positive (Hashimoto's confirmed).

notes.author: Dr. Manuel Reyes
notes.preview: 22-year-old Spanish-speaking woman presenting with 6-month history of progressive joint pain, fatigue, and hair loss. Point-of-care labs: ANA 1:80, TSH 5.32, RF negative. Levothyroxine started; rheum + PCP referrals; additional outpatient labs sent.

notes.author: Dr. James Tran
notes.preview: 19F twisted left ankle at work cleaning a hotel room. Lateral malleolus swelling, unable to bear weight. X-ray: no fracture. Grade II inversion ankle sprain. RICE, ankle brace, crutches, work note.

notes.author: Dr. Rachel Kim
notes.preview: 18F with 2 days dysuria and frequency. UA positive LE + nitrites. Uncomplicated lower UTI. Nitrofurantoin 100 mg BID x 5d (cash-pay). Patient lost Medicaid in 2020; referred to Esperanza Health FQHC for ongoing PCP.

notes.author: Dr. Linda Wong
notes.department: Family Medicine — Johns Hopkins Community Health Partnership
notes.preview: 16F f/u after iron-deficiency anemia treatment. Hgb normalized to 13.4; ferritin 38. Anemia resolved. Discontinued iron. HPV #3 given. RTC annually or PRN.

notes.author: Dr. Andrew Park
notes.preview: 15F with 2 days sore throat. T 99.1, pharyngeal erythema without exudate, mild tender anterior cervical LAD. Rapid strep negative. Likely viral pharyngitis. Supportive care.

notes.author: Dr. Linda Wong
notes.department: Family Medicine — Johns Hopkins Community Health Partnership
notes.preview: New patient intake for 15F recent immigrant from rural Guatemala. Establishing care, immunization catch-up, immigration physical. Family Hx: maternal aunt with SLE in Guatemala (disabled), mother with hypothyroidism, father deceased age 39 in MVA (had treated TB). Labs reveal iron-deficiency anemia (Hgb 10.8, ferritin 6); started ferrous sulfate. Hep B/C, HIV negative. TB IGRA indeterminate — CXR ordered.
